_doc: Batch 3 — 50 distinct, large file operation scenarios. Each is a unique transformation.

scenarios.description: Create file, write 100KB in one shot, verify size
Append 10KB then append another 10KB, total 20KB
Write at specific offset (100KB into empty file)
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"wb"); f.write(b"\0"*102400); f.seek(50000); f.write(b"MARKER"); f.close()' {vfs}/punch

scenarios.description: Truncate large file to 1KB
Write 1MB then verify MD5 against expected
Read 5MB ZIP from middle offset
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"rb"); f.seek(262144); d=f.read(1024); import hashlib; print(hashlib.md5(d).hexdigest())' {vfs}/z

scenarios.description: Write 10MB file in 100KB chunks
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"wb"); [f.write(bytes([i%256])*102400) for i in range(100)]; f.close()' {vfs}/chunked

scenarios.description: Create sparse file (seek + write at end)
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"wb"); f.seek(5000000); f.write(b"x"); f.close()' {vfs}/sparse

scenarios.description: Overwrite existing file with completely different content
Write then truncate to 0, file becomes empty
Copy DMG (large binary) and verify MD5
Write 2MB random data, verify size
Read 1KB from beginning, middle, end of large file
Append 1 byte at a time 100 times (small appends)
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"wb"); [f.write(b"a") for _ in range(100)]; f.close()' {vfs}/many_appends

scenarios.description: Copy 264MB ZIP and verify size
Write 4MB then verify MD5
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; data=b"abcdefgh"*524288; open(sys.argv[1],"wb").write(data); import hashlib; print(hashlib.md5(data).hexdigest())' {vfs}/fourmb

scenarios.description: Write 256KB content, read back exact bytes
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; data=bytes(i%256 for i in range(262144)); open(sys.argv[1],"wb").write(data)' {vfs}/exact

scenarios.description: Write 64KB random bytes, MD5 check
Create file with content, append, prepend (rewrite)
Copy large file, then change one byte, MD5 should differ
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"r+b"); f.seek(100); f.write(b"X"); f.close()' {vfs}/big

scenarios.description: Write 5MB at offset 2MB (extend file)
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"wb"); f.write(b"\0"*2097152); f.seek(2097152); f.write(b"x"*5242880); f.close()' {vfs}/extend

scenarios.description: Truncate to exactly 1 byte
Create two files of different sizes, swap them
Write 1MB zeros
Read a large PDF and verify file integrity
Create file, seek to end, write 100 more bytes
Write 4KB at offset 8KB (gap)
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"wb"); f.seek(8192); f.write(b"X"*4096); f.close()' {vfs}/gap

scenarios.description: Create two files with identical content, MD5 matches
Write 3MB, then read with dd in 512KB blocks
Create file with 8KB boundary content, MD5
Append 1MB to ZIP, MD5 reflects new content
Write content then verify with cmp against expected
Write file with very specific byte pattern, verify
Create empty file, then append 50MB
Write a 16KB file in one shot
Write 1MB content, then truncate to 100KB
Copy then verify seekable read
Write 100KB then verify first byte is correct
Append zero bytes (no-op write)
Write 500KB then read last 1KB
Create 5MB of repeating pattern, MD5 stable
Write file, get size via stat, compare
Write 32KB then 32KB, verify no data lost at boundary
Create file, mv it, then append
Write huge amount then truncate to 0 then write again
Write content using only sequential appends (no seek)
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"wb"); [f.write(str(i).encode()) for i in range(1000)]; f.close()' {vfs}/seq

scenarios.description: Copy XLSX and verify exact content
Write 1MB, then overwrite starting at byte 500000
setup: python3 -c 'import sys; f=open(sys.argv[1],"r+b"); f.seek(500000); f.write(b"OVERWRITE"); f.close()' {vfs}/x

scenarios.description: Create file with single byte, append 999999 more
Create 16MB file with deterministic content
